text:
: pediatric forms of CD at diagnosis (41)
Category
Typical features
Non-specific features
Macroscopic
-	 Aphthous ulcers of the mucosa
-	 Linear or serpiginous ulcers
-	 Cobblestone appearance
-	 Stricture or intestinal narrowing with prestenotic dilatation
-	 Intestinal wall thickening with luminal narrowing (imaging or surgery)
-	 Perianal lesions: fistulas, abscesses, anal strictures, anal canal ulcers, large inflammatory skin tag
-	 Skip lesions
-	 Jejunal or ileal ulcers
-	 Edema
-	 Erythema
-	 Friability
-	 Granularity
-	 Exudate
-	 Loss of vascular pattern
-	 Isolated aphthous ulcers
-	 Perianal lesions: midline anal fissures, small skin tag
Microscopic
-	 Non-caseating granulomas (remote from ruptured crypts)
-	 Focal chronic inflammation, transmural inflammatory infiltrate, submucosal fibrosis
-	 Granulomas adjacent to a ruptured crypt
-	 Mild nonspecific inflammatory infiltrate in the lamina propria
-	 Mucosal ulceration/erosion
-	 Signs of chronicity: architectural alterations of the crypts, cell metaplasia of
